figures.caption: Figure 4. Main results of LLaVA-1.5-7b on the MME.
Table 1. Main results on POPE tasks. The best performances are bolded.

Table 11. Results on more advanced LVLMs, including Qwen2-VL-7B (Wang et al., 2024a), InternVL2-8B (Chen et al., 2024b), LLaVA-NeXT and Qwen2.5-VL-7B (Bai et al., 2025). Cog. and Hall. denote the cognitive and hallucination subset of MME benchmark.
Table 9. Comparisons between CAST and RL works on POPE, CHAIR, and MME benchmarks.
Table 12. Comparisons on HallusionBench and GAVIE benchmarks across different MLLMs.

Table 4. We construct a caption query candidate pool (N=16), where we derive our test cases as follows: (1) four queries are randomly selected; (2) one optimal query is chosen using caption query optimization algorithm; and (3) an ensemble steering strategy is applied. VA (%) indicates the average percentage of attention weights over visual tokens when fed corresponding query. α and K denote the intensity and number of the steering. We select the optimal parameters separately for each setting.
Figure 5. The accuracies of probes (left) and ablation study of α and K on POPE (right).
Table 8. Result compared with more advanced methods on MS-COCO POPE.

Figure 2. A quantitative analysis from head-wise (a) and layer-wise (b) perspective on visual attention weights, which demonstrates that caption queries significantly enhance visual attention of LLaVA-1.5-7b.

Figure 3. An overview of the CAST method. Each square in the matrix represents the attention head output. Squares with dark green color indicate refined caption-guided attention head outputs. CAST consists of three stages: (1) Caption-Guided Attention Heads Probe §4.2: We use probing techniques to identify caption-guided attention heads, which exhibit enhanced visual attention when fed caption queries versus non-caption queries. (2) Estimation of Perception Refined Vectors §4.3: We estimate the perception refined vectors by computing the attention output shift vectors from feeding non-caption queries to caption queries. (3) Steering at Inference Time §4.4: We apply the precomputed attention refined vectors to the Top-K caption-guided attention heads during inference, thereby enhancing visual attention and activating the model’s inherent fine-grained visual perception capability and effectively mitigate object hallucination.
Figure 6. Case study of caption task on CHAIR.
Table 15. Performance comparison between SVM and LR classifiers on POPE, CHAIR, and MME benchmarks. The best results are highlighted in bold.
Figure 8. Non-caption query case of LLaVA-1.5-7b on MMHal-Bench.
Figure 7. Non-caption query case of LLaVA-1.5-7b on MMHal-Bench.
